name: display.py
summary: Provides rich ANSI-colored text formatting helpers for bird cards, bonus cards, player boards, and game state used by the CLI agent display layer.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the cards subsystem, re-exporting parsed card data from the schema and parse subpackages.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the card parsing subsystem, re-exporting the full parse pipeline including catalog, fields, loader, matchers, and power parsers.

name: catalog.py
summary: Provides stable bird and bonus card catalog accessors (count, index lookup by card ID) backed by the parsed card list.

name: fields.py
summary: Parses individual bird and bonus card fields from raw JSON records into typed schema objects (habitat, food cost, nest type, bonus thresholds, goal categories).

name: loader.py
summary: Loads and caches all bird, bonus, and round-goal card data from JSON source files, providing the singleton parsed card list used throughout the engine.

name: matchers.py
summary: Contains 40 regex-based matcher functions for brown/white bird power text, each registering a specific EffectKind pattern via the parse registry decorator.

name: pink_matchers.py
summary: Contains 5 regex-based matcher functions for pink (when-activated) bird powers, registered via the parse registry's pink_pattern decorator.

name: power.py
summary: Orchestrates bird power text parsing, extracting one or more Effect objects from raw power text by normalizing and dispatching to registered matchers.

name: registry.py
summary: Provides the pattern and pink_pattern decorator-based registry for bird power matchers, mapping regex patterns to EffectKind constructors.

name: tags.py
summary: Defines tag constants and a to_int utility for mapping string tags from raw card JSON to integer indices used in encoding.

name: schema.py
summary: Core Pydantic data models and StrEnum types for all card domain objects: Habitat, Food, NestType, EffectKind, BirdCost, Effect, Power, Bird, BonusCard, EndRoundGoal, and their raw JSON record counterparts.

name: decisions.py
summary: Defines the complete Decision/Choice type hierarchy used across the game engine: 41 Choice subclasses and corresponding Decision[C] containers covering every agent decision point from setup through end-of-round.

name: choice_encode.py
summary: Encodes agent choice vectors for every Decision subclass into fixed-size float32 tensors for the RL policy network, including board-impact deltas for scoring and bonus goals.

name: actions.py
summary: Implements the four main Wingspan turn actions (play bird, gain food, lay eggs, draw cards) as free functions taking the Engine as first argument, including helper logic for enumerating playable birds and offering birdfeeder resets.

name: core.py
summary: Defines the Engine class â€” the central game orchestrator that owns the turn loop, setup phase, Agent protocol plumbing, and ask() validation gateway.

name: helpers.py
summary: Provides food cost evaluation helpers: cost_meets checks if a food pool satisfies a bird cost, and enumerate_payments generates all valid payment combinations.

name: log_format.py
summary: Provides structured log message formatting for game events (setup, dealt hand, turn summaries) emitted by the Engine to the display agent.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the engine powers subsystem, re-exporting all bird power handler submodules.

name: dispatch.py
summary: Core power dispatching logic: routes an EffectKind to its registered handler, applies effects, offers activation veto, and implements the shared lay_one_egg_on_nest helper used by multiple powers.

name: drafting.py
summary: Power handlers for bird-drafting effects: play an additional bird here, draw-N-plus-one-draft, and draw-bonus-keep mechanics.

name: egg_trade.py
summary: Power handlers for egg-trading effects: discard an egg to gain wild food or draw cards.

name: grants.py
summary: Power handlers for the largest group of bird power effects: food gain (supply, feeder, birdfeeder), egg laying, card drawing, caching, tucking, and combination tuck-then effects.

name: multi_actor.py
summary: Power handlers for effects involving multiple players: each-player-gains-a-die-in-order and all-players-lay-egg-on-nest.

name: nest_aggregate.py
summary: Power handlers for nest-aggregation effects: lay an egg on every bird of a given nest type, gain all food of a type from the feeder, and tuck from deck with payment.

name: predator_repeat.py
summary: Power handlers for predator-hunt, move-bird-if-rightmost, repeat-brown-power, and repeat-predator-power effects.

name: registry.py
summary: Provides the @handles decorator registry that maps EffectKind variants to power handler functions, and handler_for lookup used by dispatch.

name: tray_trade.py
summary: Power handlers for tray-related effects: draw all from tray, trade wild food, and fewest-habitat conditional gains (die or card).

name: reactors.py
summary: Implements pink power reactor triggers: fires when-activated reactions from all opponent birds for lay-egg, predator success, play-bird, and gain-food events.

name: scoring.py
summary: Implements all Wingspan scoring logic: round goal evaluation and standing, bonus card qualifying counts and VP, final scoring, and per-choice score delta computation for the RL encoder.

name: state.py
summary: Defines all mutable game state models: GameState (full game), Player, Board, PlayedBird, Birdfeeder, FoodPool, and RoundGoalResult â€” the core data layer for the simulation.

name: __init__.py
summary: Top-level package initializer for the wingspan simulator; currently minimal (2 non-empty lines) but marks the root package boundary.

name: architecture.py
summary: Defines the canonical neural network topology descriptor `ModelArchitecture` and supporting parameter-counting utilities (`count_parameters`, `ParamReport`) used by `PolicyValueNet` and the HTML report to describe and size every model block.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package initializer for the backward-compatibility shim layer; imports from `v0_0` and `v0_1` to expose version-routing helpers at the `compat` namespace level.

name: v0_0.py
summary: Backward-compatibility shim for artifact version 0.0: provides the legacy choice encoding layout, `PolicyValueNetV00` subclass that uses the old flat choice encoder, and reconstruction helpers that translate old encoded tensors to the current layout.

name: v0_1.py
summary: Backward-compatibility shim for artifact version 0.1: provides the legacy card-feature matrix layout (without current bonus embeddings) and `PolicyValueNetV01` / `SetupNetV01` subclasses that override the card encoder to use the old feature matrix.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package initializer and public API facade for the encoder subsystem; re-exports `encode_state`, `encode_choices`, `card_feature_matrix`, `card_summary_matrix`, `trunk_input_dim`, `choice_input_dim`, `EncodingSpec`, `state_size`, and `choice_feature_dim` from submodules.

name: layout.py
summary: Defines `EncodingSpec` (the selector between main-game and setup encoding) and computes all top-level dimension constants â€” trunk input, choice input, choice feature, state feature, decision-type, and family counts â€” that wire together the stripe layout and the neural network input layers.

name: state_encode.py
summary: Converts a `GameState` snapshot into a flat float32 numpy array suitable for neural network input, assembling board slots, food, hand, bonus progress, round goals, birdfeeder, and decision-type stripes.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package barrel for the stripes subpackage; re-exports `StripeDescriptor`, `SubFieldDescriptor`, `VectorLayout`, and the four layout functions from sibling modules.

name: card_feature.py
summary: Produces `VectorLayout` descriptors for the card-feature stripe (all 180 bird attribute sub-fields) and the hand-encoder input stripe, used by the HTML report and the compat shim to document and validate vector shapes.

name: choice.py
summary: Defines the complete choice encoding vector layout: `raw_choice_stripe_layout` emits per-decision-class stripes with sub-field descriptors for kind, gain-food, payment, board-target, exchange, bonus/goal deltas, and setup aggregates; `choice_stripe_layout` wraps it with embedding substitution.

name: descriptors.py
summary: Defines the three Pydantic descriptor types â€” `SubFieldDescriptor`, `StripeDescriptor`, and `VectorLayout` â€” that form the self-documenting layer layout schema used by the HTML report and compat shims.

name: embed_rules.py
summary: Provides `_EmbedRule` descriptor and the four rule-set factory functions (`state_embed_rules`, `setup_embed_rules`, `choice_embed_rules`) plus `embed_layout` which applies rules to a raw `VectorLayout`, replacing card-index stripes with embedding-dimension placeholders for the network's actual input.

name: state.py
summary: Defines `state_stripe_layout`, the authoritative `VectorLayout` descriptor for the full game-state tensor covering food, board slots, board summaries, hand, bonus progress, birdfeeder, misc scalars, round goals, card indices, and decision-type stripes.

name: core.py
summary: Defines `PolicyValueNet`, the primary PyTorch model for gameplay: builds a card encoder, optional hand encoder, state trunk, per-family choice scorer, and value head from a `ModelArchitecture` config; exposes `encode_state`, `encode_choices`, and `forward` for the training/inference pipeline.

name: hand_model.py
summary: Provides four tensor-manipulation helpers (`multihot_from_indices`, `set_summary_from_indices`, `set_summary_from_multihot`, `embed_card_set`) for converting card index tensors into multi-hot or summary-row representations fed into the hand encoder.

name: mlp.py
summary: Provides `build_body` and `build_readout` factory functions that construct `nn.Sequential` MLP blocks from layer width lists, activation, dropout, and LayerNorm settings, used by all neural network components in the project.

name: html.py
summary: Generates a self-contained HTML architecture report page from the model's encoding layouts, architecture config, and parameter count report, including stripe tables, architecture diagram (embedded SVG), and parameter breakdown.

name: svg.py
summary: Renders the neural-network architecture as an inline SVG diagram with labeled blocks, op rows (Linear/LayerNorm/Activation/Dropout), IO boxes, connector bands between rows, and parameter count badges; entry point is `build_arch_svg`.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package initializer and public API barrel for the setup model subsystem; re-exports `SetupEncoding`, `SetupArchitecture`, `setup_readout_input_dim`, `count_setup_parameters`, `SetupNet`, `generate`, and `record` from submodules.

name: architecture.py
summary: Defines `SetupEncoding` (the feature vector structure for game-setup candidates) and `SetupArchitecture` (the neural network config for the setup net), plus `count_setup_parameters` for parameter reporting.

name: candidates.py
summary: Defines `SetupCandidate` (the typed representation of one possible initial-setup selection) and `enumerate_setup_candidates` / `select_by_margins` for generating and sampling from the combinatorial space of card+food+bonus choices.

name: encode.py
summary: Converts a `SetupCandidate` and game context into a fixed-length float32 feature vector for the setup net, encoding kept cards, food, bonus affinity, tray indices, birdfeeder state, and round-goal affinity stripes.

name: generate.py
summary: Provides `RandomSetupGenerator`, which samples training-data setup instances by randomly dealing and selecting initial cards and food, using softmax-weighted sampling to create realistic and diverse training data.

name: record.py
summary: Provides `SetupSample` (training record with features, margin, and candidate array) and `SetupDataStore` (append-only JSONL file store with iteration, count, and clear operations) for setup model training data persistence.

name: stripes.py
summary: Produces annotated `VectorLayout` descriptors for the setup candidate input vector (`setup_stripe_layout`) and the setup-net readout input after embedding substitution (`setup_readout_stripe_layout`), mirroring the structure of `encode/stripes/state.py` for the setup domain.

name: setup_learner.py
summary: Implements offline (batch-MSE) and online (per-game actor-critic) training update functions for the setup net: `offline_fit` trains from a stored JSONL dataset, `online_update` from a live sample list, and `actor_critic_update` runs a policy-gradient + value loss update over grouped samples.

name: setup_net.py
summary: Defines `SetupNet`, the PyTorch model for predicting setup quality margins: builds a frozen card encoder, an optional hand encoder, and a readout MLP (with optional policy head for actor-critic mode) from `SetupArchitecture` and `ModelArchitecture` configs.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package init for the cloud module that re-exports all public symbols from monitor, runfile, runner, s3sync, and status submodules.

name: __main__.py
summary: CLI entry point for the cloud module that parses arguments, loads a CloudRunFile (optionally from S3), applies overrides, and launches HeadlessRunner for a remote training run.

name: monitor.py
summary: Live terminal dashboard that polls S3 for RunStatus snapshots from all active cloud training runs and renders them in a Rich table with status LEDs, ETA, and win-rate columns.

name: runfile.py
summary: Pydantic models and parser for the cloud run configuration file, defining S3Config, SyncConfig, and CloudRunFile structures that drive headless training runs.

name: runner.py
summary: HeadlessRunner orchestrates a cloud training session: restores from S3, launches a training thread, supervises progress, periodically uploads checkpoints and game chunks, publishes status snapshots, and handles graceful shutdown on signals.

name: s3sync.py
summary: S3Sync class and utility functions for downloading runs, uploading checkpoints and game chunks, fetching text objects, and listing run statuses from an S3 bucket backing cloud training.

name: status.py
summary: RunStatus Pydantic model and build_status factory that computes a rich training status snapshot from RunState, including win rate, ETA, games-per-second, and evaluation metrics.

name: loaders.py
summary: Checkpoint loading functions for PolicyValueNet and SetupNet, with compat shim routing for v0.0 and v0.1 artifact formats, encoding-key validation, and net reconstruction from run-dir metadata.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package init for the reporting module that re-exports the inspect_cli entry point.

name: inspect_cli.py
summary: Rich terminal CLI for inspecting trained model checkpoints: prints state-encoding layout, choice-encoding stripes, architecture diagram, parameter counts, and optionally writes an HTML report.

name: dashboard.py
summary: Rich-based live terminal dashboard for tournament mode: builds a four-panel layout (header, standings, events, progress) and renders real-time stats from a TournamentLive object.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package init for the training module, re-exporting the main app entry point and serving as the module barrel for consumers like the cloud runner.

name: __main__.py
summary: Minimal Python __main__ that invokes the training app entry point when the training package is run directly via python -m wingspan.training.

name: app.py
summary: Training application entry point: parses CLI args, builds TrainConfig, launches a TrainingLoop in a worker thread, runs the Rich terminal dashboard, and prints a completion summary.

name: artifacts.py
summary: Utility functions for generating canonical checkpoint and final-eval artifact filenames from iteration numbers, used by the training loop for consistent artifact naming.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package init for the training charts subpackage, re-exporting braille canvas, convergence chart, histogram, and inset renderable classes.

name: braille.py
summary: BrailleCanvas class for terminal-based dot-matrix plotting using Unicode Braille characters, with a Bresenham line-drawing algorithm for rendering line series.

name: convergence_chart.py
summary: GettingBetterChart Rich renderable that displays a side-by-side Braille convergence chart with win-rate and score/margin plot blocks, axis labels, inset histograms, and phase markers for the training dashboard.

name: geometry.py
summary: Chart size constants defining pixel-to-braille-cell dimensions and layout proportions used across convergence and inset chart renderers.

name: histogram.py
summary: FamilyHistogram Rich renderable displaying a bar chart of decision-family usage counts for the training dashboard inset, with color-coded tier bars and a total-decisions footer.

name: insets.py
summary: Renders rich text inset and strip widgets for the training dashboard, displaying eval and collect phase metrics (win-rate EWMA, margin, game counts) as formatted terminal panels.

name: text_helpers.py
summary: Provides low-level text rendering utilities for terminal chart components: Unicode sparkline generation, eighth-block bar rendering, human-readable number formatting, and multi-column layout joining.

name: arch_diagram.py
summary: Renders an interactive and static terminal architecture diagram of the PolicyValueNet and SetupNet, showing layer shapes, parameter counts, and data flow using Rich Text box-drawing primitives.

name: convergence.py
summary: Computes EWMA series and axis ranges for training convergence charts, including win-rate, score, and margin curves with regime-aware data point handling.

name: dashboard.py
summary: Implements the real-time terminal training dashboard using Rich layouts, rendering header stats, production metrics, learning charts, health indicators, and a pause/command panel.

name: loop.py
summary: Defines the TrainingLoop class orchestrating the RL self-play training cycle: game collection, model updates, evaluation, checkpointing, and system monitoring across all training phases.

name: loop_checkpoint.py
summary: Handles iteration-level checkpointing and artifact persistence: saving model weights, game history logs, metrics, and writing final run summaries to disk atomically.

name: loop_eval.py
summary: Manages periodic evaluation against a frozen opponent, opponent promotion logic (graduation from random phase, advancing to stronger opponent snapshots), and eval result recording.

name: loop_resume.py
summary: Handles training run resumption from checkpoints: loading saved weights and optimizer state, initializing phase, target, and history logs for fresh or resumed runs.

name: loop_setup.py
summary: Manages setup phase logic for each training iteration: determining if setup phase is active, running offline setup network fitting, syncing embedder weights between main and setup nets, and resuming from setup checkpoints.

name: loop_target.py
summary: Handles the target iteration event: checking if a target iteration has been reached, pausing to query the user for a new target, and resuming the loop after the response.

name: metrics.py
summary: Defines all Pydantic data models for training metrics: score breakdowns, family counts, eval results, EWMA accumulators, iteration metrics, game outcomes, and system stats.

name: metrics_log.py
summary: Reads and caches the per-iteration metrics log from disk (JSONL format), providing the iteration history as a list of IterationMetrics for chart and convergence computations.

name: runmeta.py
summary: Manages run metadata persistence: writing and reading model configs, session records, inspect reports, and HTML model summaries; also provides layout and dimension reporting utilities used by the dashboard and arch diagram.

name: runstate.py
summary: Defines RunState â€” the central mutable state object for a training run â€” tracking game history, EWMA metrics, timing, eval history, events, and progress; includes Phase and TrainingPhase enums.

name: setup_runmeta.py
summary: Provides the SetupConfig Pydantic model and functions to write and read setup model configuration artifacts alongside setup checkpoints.

name: sysmon.py
summary: Implements SystemMonitor, a background sampler that periodically reads CPU, RAM, and GPU utilization metrics using psutil and pynvml for display in the training dashboard health panel.

name: theme.py
summary: Provides color theming utilities for the training dashboard: lerp/gradient color generation, gradient-rendered text, and win-rate-dependent hero color selection.

name: version.py
summary: Defines the Version model and artifact compatibility logic: parsing version strings, checking artifact compatibility against MODEL_VERSION, and adapting old encoding specs via compat shims.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the agents subpackage, re-exporting the primary agent constructors (random_agent and cli_agent) for use throughout the training and play pipelines.

name: base.py
summary: Defines the reference random agent â€” a factory returning a closure that satisfies the Agent protocol by sampling uniformly from the offered choices using a provided RNG.

name: cli.py
summary: Provides the CLI agent factory and its supporting formatting/input helpers, enabling a human player to interactively select choices at the terminal with rendered board state.

name: interactive.py
summary: Low-level interactive terminal UI library providing keyboard-driven multi-section selection forms with ANSI rendering, used by the CLI agent for setup card/food picking.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the engine subpackage, re-exporting the Engine class from the core module for use across training, evaluation, and play components.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the model subpackage, re-exporting the core PolicyValueNet and related types for use across the training pipeline.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the players subpackage, re-exporting the player factory, spec parser, and loaders that assemble agents for use in training and evaluation.

name: factory.py
summary: Constructs typed agent callables from PlayerSpec configurations, wiring together policy networks, setup models, and logging for use in training collection and self-play evaluation.

name: spec.py
summary: Defines the PlayerKind enum and PlayerSpec Pydantic model, plus a parser that converts a raw string specification (random/cli/checkpoint path) into a typed PlayerSpec.

name: batched_collect.py
summary: Implements batched inference self-play collection: a _BatchInferenceServer accumulates per-step inference requests from concurrent game threads and processes them as a single GPU batch, amortizing inference overhead.

name: collect.py
summary: Core single-process game collection logic: plays individual Wingspan games with optional setup-phase tree sampling, recording per-step state/choice/action tuples as training data.

name: evaluate.py
summary: Evaluation harness that runs a trained policy network against an opponent or in self-play, computing win margins and summary statistics for use in the training loop quality gate.

name: learner.py
summary: Implements the REINFORCE/actor-critic gradient update step: flattens collected game records, bucketizes steps by choice count for efficient batched forward passes, and applies policy + value loss with gradient clipping.

name: loop_collect.py
summary: Training-loop collection orchestrator that dispatches game collection to multiprocess or sequential modes depending on configuration, routing setup-phase and standard games and recording results into the training loop state.

name: loop_metrics.py
summary: Computes and assembles per-iteration training metrics from collected game records, including win rates, score breakdowns, family decision counts, and setup margins for dashboard reporting.

name: mp_collect.py
summary: Multiprocess game collection engine: a ProcessPoolExecutor-backed ProcessCollector spawns worker processes for self-play, setup-phase games, and evaluation, broadcasting updated weights via atomic temp-file saves.

name: policy.py
summary: Policy action-selection utilities wrapping the PolicyValueNet: provides sample_action (stochastic), greedy_action (argmax), greedy_agent (full agent closure), and raw logit/probability extraction functions.

name: steps.py
summary: Defines the Step Pydantic model, the atomic unit of training data capturing the encoded state vector, choice feature matrix, chosen action index, player identity, and decision family index for one agent decision.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package barrel for the tournament subsystem, re-exporting key types and functions from app, config, participants, results, runner, schedule, and state modules.

name: app.py
summary: CLI entry point for running a Wingspan AI tournament, supporting both live (rich dashboard) and quiet modes with result summarization and optional JSON report export.

name: config.py
summary: Defines TournamentConfig, the Pydantic model holding all tournament settings including participants, ELO parameters, seeding, output path, and per-pair game count.

name: elo.py
summary: Self-contained ELO rating implementation providing EloTable (track and update ratings) and a replay function to re-compute ratings from a sequence of game results.

name: participants.py
summary: Defines ParticipantSpec/ParticipantKind/RunOption models and utilities for discovering, loading, and deduplicating tournament competitors from checkpoint directories.

name: picker.py
summary: Interactive terminal UI for selecting tournament participants from discovered training runs, with keyboard navigation, multi-select, and rich rendering.

name: results.py
summary: Defines result data models (GameResult, MatchupResult, ParticipantResult, TournamentReport) and the aggregate function that computes standings, matchup stats, and ELO from completed games.

name: runner.py
summary: Orchestrates tournament execution by building a schedule, dispatching games either in-process or via a ProcessPoolExecutor, and collecting GameResult objects for aggregation.

name: schedule.py
summary: Generates a round-robin tournament schedule as a list of GameTask objects, assigning deterministic per-game seeds from a base seed, pair index, and round index.

name: state.py
summary: Manages live tournament state for dashboard display, tracking ELO history, per-participant records, progress, throughput, and phase transitions during an ongoing run.

name: config.py
summary: Defines TrainConfig, the authoritative Pydantic model for all training hyperparameters covering the main network, setup model, ELO evaluation, encoding dims, and architecture key derivation.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package barrel for the training configurator sub-package, re-exporting from controller, fields, runs, screen, and state modules.

name: controller.py
summary: Implements the input-handling controller for the interactive training configurator, dispatching keyboard events to navigate fields, edit values, confirm actions, and launch/archive training runs.

name: fields.py
summary: Defines the FieldSpec type hierarchy (IntField, FloatField, ChoiceField, LayersField, etc.) and all TrainConfig field descriptors, plus read/format/commit/nudge operations for the configurator UI.

name: keys.py
summary: Cross-platform keyboard input abstraction providing KeyReader (context manager), KeyEvent/KeyKind models, and decode helpers for Windows msvcrt and POSIX termios input.

name: runs.py
summary: Provides RunSummary inspection, archive/clear operations, and status resolution for training run checkpoint directories, supporting the configurator's run management workflow.

name: screen.py
summary: Renders the full interactive training configuration screen using Rich, building the form panel, architecture summary, run info, field detail panel, and footer from ConfiguratorState.

name: state.py
summary: Defines ConfiguratorState and related Pydantic models (ConfirmPrompt, ConfirmOption) representing the complete view-model for the training configurator TUI.

name: cli.py
summary: CLI entry point for running Wingspan games, parsing command-line arguments, configuring players, initializing instrumentation, and executing one or more game loops.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package entry point for the instrumentation subpackage, re-exporting the key public names (Instrumentation, config, events, handlers, registry) for convenient access.

name: config.py
summary: Defines RunContext (run metadata Pydantic model) and InstrumentationConfig (YAML-driven config that validates handler assignments, resolves handler instances from the registry, and builds a live Instrumentation object).

name: dispatcher.py
summary: Defines the Instrumentation class, the central event dispatcher that routes game lifecycle events to registered callback handlers, supporting open/close lifecycle and 14 distinct game-event methods.

name: events.py
summary: Defines EventName enum (14 game lifecycle events) and a Protocol class for each event type, establishing the typed callback interface that all instrumentation handlers must implement.

name: __init__.py
summary: Package barrel for the handlers subpackage, importing card_visits and decision_logger submodules to trigger their registry self-registration side effects.

name: card_visits.py
summary: Implements CardVisitRecorder, an instrumentation handler that counts how many times each bird card is played across games and writes the results as JSONL to a configured output path.

name: decision_logger.py
summary: Implements DecisionLogger, an instrumentation handler that logs each agent decision (type, choice count, selected label) as a JSONL record to a configured output file.

name: registry.py
summary: Provides a name-keyed registry of instrumentation handler classes, exposing a @register decorator for handlers to self-register and lookup functions used by InstrumentationConfig to resolve handler types from YAML config.

name: Dockerfile
summary: Single-stage Amazon Linux 2023 Docker image that installs CPU-only PyTorch and the wingspan package, then runs the cloud training entrypoint (`wingspan.cloud`) as an unprivileged user. Designed for CPU-only self-play training with artifacts shipped to S3.

name: .dockerignore
summary: Docker build-context filter that restricts the image build to only `pyproject.toml`, `README.md`, and `src/`, explicitly excluding VCS metadata, virtual environments, checkpoints, tests, docs, and large artifact files to keep the image lean.

name: CLAUDE.md
summary: Project-specific guidance for AI coding agents, covering the worktree-based change workflow, architectural patterns to preserve (Decision/Choice system, Engine orchestrator, Agent protocol, bird power dispatcher), test conventions, and things to avoid. Serves as the authoritative contract between the codebase and any AI-assisted contributor.

name: README.md
summary: Primary user-facing documentation covering setup, all CLI commands (play, dashboard, tournament, inspect, cloud, monitor), and pointers to deeper docs. The 8-section guide is the main entry point for new developers and users of the Wingspan simulator.

name: TODO.md
summary: Active task list tracking open work items across four areas: game-log display improvements for `wingspan play --log`, a training feature to bootstrap against a pre-trained opponent, a modeling change to use one-hots for round/cube counts, and general investigation topics requiring research reports.

name: cards.csv
summary: Master data table for all 180 Wingspan core-set bird cards, with one row per card capturing name, power text, point value, food costs by type, nest type flags, habitat flags, wingspan, egg limit, card color, and feature flags used by bonus cards and encoded as input features for the RL model.

name: coverage_baseline.txt
summary: Single-line file storing the current test coverage baseline percentage (85%), used by the quality gate script as a one-way ratchet â€” the gate fails if measured coverage drops below this value.

name: main.html
summary: Baseline HTML report for the neural network model architecture and parameter summary, generated by the `wingspan inspect` command. Presents layer-by-layer parameter counts, architecture diagrams, and decision-head tables in a styled single-page dashboard with interactive collapsible sections.

name: pyproject.toml
summary: Python package configuration defining the wingspan package (v0.1.0, Python 3.12+), runtime dependencies (PyTorch, Pydantic, rich, boto3), dev-only tooling (pytest, pyright, black, isort), the `wingspan` CLI entry point, and strict Pyright/black/isort/coverage settings used by the quality gate.

name: README.md
summary: Comprehensive guide (6 sections) for running Wingspan self-play training unattended in a container: describes the cloud architecture (S3-persisted, Spot-interruptible), image build steps, local smoke-test instructions, ECS Fargate Spot launch procedure, and the live monitoring command.

name: ecs-task-def.json
summary: Standalone AWS ECS Fargate task definition for the wingspan-trainer container: allocates 2 vCPU / 8 GB, wires execution and task IAM roles, routes logs to CloudWatch under /wingspan/trainer, and sets a 120-second stop timeout for graceful Spot-reclaim handling.

name: main.tf
summary: Terraform configuration that provisions the full durable AWS infrastructure for cloud training: an S3 bucket for run persistence, an ECS cluster with Fargate Spot as the default capacity provider, IAM execution and task roles scoped to the bucket prefix, a CloudWatch log group, and the ECS task definition for the trainer container.

name: aws_s3_bucket.runs
summary: S3 bucket that stores all training run artifacts: checkpoints, metrics, game logs, and status files, keyed under a configurable prefix.

name: aws_ecs_cluster.wingspan
summary: ECS cluster named 'wingspan' that hosts trainer tasks, configured to prefer Fargate Spot capacity for cost-efficient preemptible training.

name: aws_ecs_task_definition.trainer
summary: Fargate task definition for the wingspan-trainer container: 2 vCPU / 8 GB, awsvpc networking, 120-second stop timeout, CloudWatch log driver, wired to execution and task IAM roles.

name: aws_iam_role.task
summary: IAM role assumed by the running trainer container, granting scoped S3 GetObject, PutObject, and ListBucket permissions on the runs bucket.

name: aws_iam_role.task_execution
summary: ECS task execution IAM role granting Fargate the AmazonECSTaskExecutionRolePolicy permissions needed to pull the container image from ECR and ship logs to CloudWatch.

name: run.example.yaml
summary: Example cloud training run-file that configures a single named experiment: S3 bucket/prefix/region for persistence, sync cadences (status heartbeat, checkpoint upload, game-log chunking), and a full TrainConfig block with hyperparameters for a 5000-iteration self-play run.

name: BIRDS.md
summary: Exhaustive reference for all 180 bird power implementations: maps each EffectKind variant through its parser matcher, engine handler, and any known deviations or gaps. Organized into 27 power categories (food, eggs, cards, tuck, predator, pink reactions, etc.) with per-bird call-out tables and cross-cutting conventions.

name: BONUSES.md
summary: Documents all 26 bonus cards and 16 end-of-round goals: acquisition rules, scoring formulas, state-encoding stripes, choice-encoding deltas, and groupings by category. Covers dynamic cards (Breeding Manager, Ecologist, Oologist, Visionary Leader) separately and notes known scoring gaps.

name: DECISIONS.md
summary: Defines the full Decision/Choice taxonomy for the RL agent: 13 decision families (MAIN_ACTION through SETUP), their choice-vector stripe layouts, ALL_DECISION_CLASSES ordering rules, and the optional-then-commit flow. Authoritative reference for adding or reordering decision points.

name: PROJECT.md
summary: Top-level package map for the Wingspan simulator: lists what is modelled (180 birds, 26 bonus cards, 16 goals), the root-level modules, and links to each subpackage's INDEX.md for detailed module listings.

name: QUALITY.md
summary: Reference for the quality_gate.sh CI script: describes the gate's phases (pyright, isort, black, pytest, coverage), exit codes (0 pass / 1 code problem / 2 infra failure), targeted section flags for partial runs, and the coverage ratchet mechanism.

name: RESEARCH.md
summary: Research agenda cataloguing planned analytical projects (card power rankings, model interpretation, score distribution analysis) with per-project feasibility verdicts and an infrastructure gap analysis updated June 2026. Identifies which projects are runnable today vs. need new harnesses.

name: TRAINING.md
summary: Authoritative training program document covering the full RL self-play pipeline: actor-critic algorithm selection, epoch sizing, throughput profiling (collection vs. update bottleneck), GPU memory optimization (padding elimination), checkpointing strategy, architecture scaling guidance, phased roadmap (Phase 0â€“3), and MLOps logging recommendations.

name: VERSIONING.md
summary: Defines the artifact versioning contract for model checkpoints: FRESH vs. REGIME change taxonomy, MODEL_VERSION changelog (v0.0 through v0.2), compat shim rules, MINOR/MAJOR bump triggers, and the load+play guarantee that every versioned checkpoint must satisfy.

name: auto_merge_worktree.sh
summary: Automated worktree merge loop that calls merge_worktree.sh repeatedly and invokes the Claude CLI to diagnose and fix quality-gate failures or git conflicts, retrying up to MAX_RETRIES times before giving up.

name: create_worktree.sh
summary: Creates a named git worktree on a 'wt/<slug>' branch from the current HEAD of main, installs a fresh virtual environment inside the worktree, and writes a merge-authorization lock file to prevent premature merges.

name: merge_worktree.sh
summary: Squash-merges a completed feature worktree into main after verifying the merge-auth lock is absent; refreshes the main venv if pyproject.toml changed, runs the quality gate with coverage, commits, pushes, and cleans up the worktree and branch.

name: quality_gate.sh
summary: The single sanctioned quality gate for the project: runs strict pyright type-checking, isort + black formatting, a second pyright pass post-format, and pytest (parallel by default via pytest-xdist); optionally measures coverage and enforces a one-way ratchet against coverage_baseline.txt.

name: bonus.json
summary: Complete definition database for all 26 Wingspan bonus cards, including scoring conditions, VP thresholds, rulings, and expansion set membership. Each entry carries human-readable condition text, percentile statistics, and official FAQ rulings used by the engine's bonus scoring logic.

name: general.json
summary: Indexed collection of 34 official Wingspan game rulings and clarifications, each with a name, explanatory text, and source URL. Provides the authoritative text for edge-case mechanics such as pink power timing, end-of-round goal counting, and birdfeeder reroll rules used across the engine.

name: goals.json
summary: Full definitions for all 16 Wingspan round-goal tiles used in 2-player games, including goal text, expansion set, per-rank VP scores (ranks 1-4 for up to 4 players), reverse-side goal text, and numeric tile and goal IDs. Consumed by the engine's round-goal scoring and encoding layers.

name: master.json
summary: Canonical database of all 180 Wingspan bird cards (plus expansion cards), containing every attribute the engine and encoder need: habitat eligibility, food costs, nest type, egg limit, wingspan, victory points, power text, color, bonus-card qualifiers, rulings, and set membership. This is the single source of truth for card definitions loaded by the card parser.

name: parameters.json
summary: Minimal runtime display-parameter configuration with a single key controlling whether bonus-card match symbols are shown in the UI. Provides default values that can be overridden at runtime.

name: README.md
summary: Documents the v0.0 backwards-compatibility fixture set: a pinned snapshot of a real training run's artifacts (model and setup checkpoints plus JSON descriptors) used by test_compat_v0_0.py to verify that artifact version 0.0 files still load and run games under the current code. Explains the Git LFS storage strategy, the deliberate absence of a version key in the JSON descriptors (to exercise the default-to-0.0 code path), and the policy for capturing future fixture sets on MINOR version bumps.

name: model_config.json
summary: Pinned v0.0 main network architecture descriptor used as a backwards-compatibility test fixture. Captures the encoding dimensions (state_dim=771, choice_dim=397), the 12-family decision ordering, and the full ModelArchitecture topology (2-layer trunk/choice heads at width 128, card embedding dim 64, shared card encoder, distinct hand model, tray set embedding) as it existed before the version field was introduced.

name: setup_config.json
summary: Pinned v0.0 setup model configuration fixture recording the setup network architecture (setup_feature_dim=308, two-layer MLP at 128/64) plus the full main architecture descriptor used alongside the setup net. Used by test_compat_v0_0.py to confirm the setup checkpoint loads and plays correctly under current code.

name: README.md
summary: Documents the v0.1 backwards-compatibility fixture set: a pinned snapshot of a real training run captured when MODEL_VERSION was bumped to 0.1, used by tests/test_compat_v0_1.py to guarantee that v0.1 artifacts continue to load and play games under future code. Describes the capture methodology, Git LFS storage of compressed checkpoints, and the policy for retaining fixture sets across MINOR version bumps.

name: model_config.json
summary: Pinned v0.1 main network architecture configuration used as a backwards-compatibility test fixture. Captures the 12-family decision ordering, state_dim=771, choice_dim=215, and the full ModelArchitecture descriptor (trunk/choice/head layers, card embedding, distinct hand model, tray set embedding) that defines the v0.1 encoding contract.

name: setup_config.json
summary: Pinned v0.1 setup network configuration used as a backwards-compatibility test fixture alongside model_config.json. Records setup_feature_dim=308, the setup model's hidden-layer architecture, and an embedded copy of the main network architecture descriptor, all tagged with version: "0.1".

name: .gitattributes
summary: Git line-ending configuration ensuring shell scripts are always checked out with LF endings to prevent CRLF corruption when running quality gate scripts on Windows.

name: .understandignore
summary: Exclusion patterns for the understand-anything analysis tool, listing generated directories, test artifacts, data files, and virtual environments to skip during codebase indexing.

name: export_card_attrs.py
summary: Developer script that exports all 180 core-set bird cards as CSV rows with 44 unnormalized attribute columns (food costs, nest type, habitats, exchange vectors), useful for external analysis and card power research.

name: check_mp_collect.py
summary: Diagnostic script that benchmarks the multiprocessing game collector (ProcessCollector) by running multiple collector workers and reporting throughput, game counts, and validation checks.

name: gen_arch_experiment.py
summary: Script that generates a set of YAML run-config files for architecture ablation experiments, sweeping hyperparameter combinations and optionally printing a tournament command to compare results.

name: measure_ipc.py
summary: Diagnostic script that measures inter-process communication overhead by benchmarking pickle serialization/deserialization of game records and analyzing state-vector sparsity.

name: profile_collection.py
summary: Profiling harness that measures sequential vs. batched game collection throughput, patches encode functions with a timer context manager, and runs cProfile attribution to identify encoding bottlenecks.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index documenting the agents subpackage modules, including the Agent protocol, random agent reference implementation, and neural-network agent wrappers.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index documenting the cards subpackage, covering immutable bird/bonus/goal card definitions, enumerations, and the parse subpackage for JSON loading.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the cards.parse subpackage describing the JSON loader, power-text regex matchers, and EffectKind registry that converts raw text into structured card data.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the cloud subpackage documenting containerized training run entry points, S3 persistence helpers, and the cloud monitor module.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the encode subpackage describing state and choice tensor encoders, layout constants, and the stripes subpackage for programmatic stripe registration.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the encode.stripes subpackage documenting the programmatic stripe registry that maps game-state dimensions to tensor offsets.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the engine subpackage documenting the core Engine class, actions, reactors, scoring, collection utilities, and the powers subpackage for bird-power dispatch.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the engine.powers subpackage documenting the EffectKind dispatcher, brown/white/pink power handler modules, and the UNIMPLEMENTED fallback.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the instrumentation subpackage documenting event-callback hooks, the callback registry, and the handlers subpackage for game-event observers.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the model subpackage documenting the PolicyValueNet architecture, ModelArchitecture descriptor, and checkpoint loading utilities.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the players subpackage documenting CLI seat-spec parsing utilities that instantiate the correct Agent for each player slot.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the reporting subpackage documenting model introspection utilities and HTML report generation for training runs.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the setup_model subpackage documenting the value-regression bandit used for the bird-selection setup phase, separate from the main policy-value network.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Per-package index for the tournament subpackage documenting the round-robin tournament runner, match scheduling, and result aggregation modules.

name: __main__.py
summary: Unified CLI dispatcher for the wingspan package, routing sub-commands (play, dashboard, tournament, cloud, monitor, inspect) to their respective entry-point functions via a match statement.

name: introspect.py
summary: Model introspection module providing utilities to inspect and report on the trained PolicyValueNet, referenced in __main__.py but absent from the current working tree (possibly relocated to reporting.inspect_cli).

name: report.py
summary: HTML report generation module for training run summaries and game statistics, absent from the current working tree (possibly relocated to the reporting subpackage).

name: report_svg.py
summary: SVG-based report generation helper for visual game statistics output, absent from the current working tree (possibly relocated to the reporting subpackage).

name: INDEX.md
summary: Module index for the training.configure subpackage, describing the interactive 'FLIGHT PLAN' configurator that guides users through setting up training runs.

name: INDEX.md
summary: Comprehensive module index for the training subpackage, covering entry points, config/metadata, training loop orchestrator, collection, learning, evaluation, metrics, dashboard, and subpackages across 9 sections.

name: conftest.py
summary: Pytest configuration providing test collection ordering (pool-based tests last) and a session-scoped fixture that resets PyTorch thread count after tests to avoid resource contention.

name: test_activation_log.py
summary: Test suite for the activation log system, verifying that row activation headers, power dispatch logging, forced feeder take logging, and single-choice auto-resolution are all recorded correctly.

name: test_birdfeeder.py
summary: Comprehensive test suite for the birdfeeder mechanics â€” reroll distribution, choice-die semantics, feeder reset offers (auto and prompted), and the main gain-food action's interaction with reset decisions.

name: test_birds_no_eggs_integration.py
summary: Integration tests for bird-card state encoding and egg-laying mechanics when birds have zero egg capacity, covering state encoding, goal identity visibility, choice pricing, and anti-egg goal skip behavior.

name: test_bonus_categories.py
summary: Test suite for bonus card category scoring â€” tagged bird matching, per-bird payouts, tiered scoring, multiple-card summation, and setup help line formatting.

name: test_bonus_linear_value.py
summary: Tests for the bonus card linear value interpolation helpers, verifying monotonicity, flat-tail behavior, and consistency between player-form scoring and the count-based linear value functions.

name: test_card_table_cache.py
summary: Tests for the PolicyValueNet card-table inference cache, verifying cache population on eval(), consistency between training and eval forwards, weight-reload invalidation, and gradient flow in training mode.

name: test_choice_landing_slot.py
summary: Tests that choice encoding rows correctly embed landing-slot information for play-bird, payment, and setup decision types, and verifies the model's candidate/kept-set embedding mechanics.

name: test_cloud_runfile.py
summary: Tests for cloud run-file parsing, covering run-name injection, scratch-dir defaults, explicit checkpoint-dir preservation, bucket validation, S3 URI detection, and final artifact name generation.

name: test_cloud_s3sync.py
summary: Tests for the S3Sync offload mechanism, verifying that game chunk uploads trim to the last complete newline and are skipped when no complete line is present.

name: test_cloud_status.py
summary: Tests for the cloud status builder, verifying progress-and-opponent fields and final-eval stats serialization in the training run status object.

name: test_commit_row_pricing.py
summary: Tests for choice-row delta/pricing stripes in the encoder, verifying hand-growth, egg-capacity, accept-exchange, and bonus-delta fields on main-action and accept-row encoded choices.

name: test_compat_shim_v0_0.py
summary: Tests for the v0.0 compat shim â€” verifying frozen encoding dims match the pinned fixture era, row-by-row encoding parity between legacy and live encoders, and version-predicate routing for PolicyValueNet construction.

name: test_compat_v0_0.py
summary: End-to-end compatibility tests loading gzipped v0.0 fixture checkpoints, verifying model-config reading, state-dict loading, forward pass validity, full game play, and choice-layout routing to the frozen registry.

name: test_compat_v0_1.py
summary: End-to-end compatibility tests loading gzipped v0.1 fixture checkpoints, verifying explicit version fields, compat-shim classification, forward pass, full game play, and choice-layout routing to the live registry.

name: test_convergence_charts.py
summary: Tests for convergence chart logic â€” score-margin windowing, EWMA series for win-rate/margin/score, opponent-change marker columns, round-trip serialization of change iterations, and Rich dashboard rendering with long histories.

name: test_engine_agent_dispatch.py
summary: Tests for Engine agent registration and dispatch: verifies agent-by-player-id indexing, error on unset agent, single-choice auto-resolution without consulting the agent, and deepcopy preservation of agent routing.

name: test_evaluate_core.py
summary: Tests for the core evaluation module: covers summarize_eval statistics, evaluate_vs_opponent progress callbacks, and run_final_self_play_eval return shape against a live PolicyValueNet.

name: test_extra_play.py
summary: Tests for the extra-play credit mechanic: verifies the play menu is offered and completes the bird placement, handles the no-legal-play waste case, the decline path, and habitat-restricted credit filtering.

name: test_food_payment.py
summary: Comprehensive tests for the food payment helpers (cost_meets and enumerate_payments): covers exact specific costs, wild substitution, two-for-one exchanges, free costs, overpay rejection, and multi-wild enumeration.

name: test_gain_food_choice.py
summary: Tests for the gain-food-choice bird power (food-choice and die-any variants): verifies parser recognition, no-collision with birdfeeder, correct agent interaction when food types are available or absent, and feeder refill on die_any.

name: test_habitat_action_track.py
summary: Extensive tests for all three habitat action tracks (forest/gain-food, grassland/lay-eggs, wetland/draw-cards): verifies count tables, trade-space gating, commit/discard/spend flows, and board render correctness.

name: test_inspect_era.py
summary: Tests for the run-era inspection and architecture diagram rendering: verifies static render output, frozen era widths, HTML/JSON descriptor round-trips, the inspect CLI, and error on missing descriptor.

name: test_instrumentation_config.py
summary: Tests for InstrumentationConfig serialization: covers model_dump, JSON, and YAML round-trips, default empty state, shared-instance behavior on build(), and correct nesting under TrainConfig.

name: test_instrumentation_dispatch.py
summary: Tests for the instrumentation event dispatch system: defines a _RecordingHandler that captures all lifecycle events, then verifies correct event sequencing, decision filtering, uninstrumented-game safety, and per-handler open/close counts.

name: test_instrumentation_validation.py
summary: Tests for InstrumentationConfig validation: verifies that unknown handler classes, missing class keys, illegal event assignments, and references to undeclared handlers all raise validation errors.

name: test_main_action.py
summary: Tests for the main-action turn loop: verifies that all three habitat actions are always offered, play-bird appears as an option, play-bird opens a valid play menu, and the payment menu follows with cost-meeting payments.

name: test_metrics_log.py
summary: Tests for the metrics log reader: covers missing-file empty return, full row reads, fingerprint-based cache hits, cache invalidation on file growth, and graceful skipping of truncated final lines.

name: test_model_and_self_play.py
summary: Core model and self-play integration tests: verifies PolicyValueNet forward shapes and masking, single-choice handling, self-play record generation, gradient updates, per-choice feature sensitivity, family-head routing, card embedding sharing, and choice-candidate representation invariants.

name: test_move_goal_delta.py
summary: Tests for move-goal delta encoding in the choice featurizer: verifies bird-count, egg-count, set membership, habitat-spread bonus deltas, and that missing move context raises a validation error.

name: test_mp_collect.py
summary: Tests for the multiprocess game collection system (ProcessCollector): verifies games are collected with correct step counts, same-seed determinism, empty-seed no-op, and sequential/parallel evaluation parity against random and frozen opponents.

name: test_players_factory.py
summary: Tests for the players factory module: covers resolve_split_setup_bonus regime detection, split/combined flag gating, disagreeing-regime error, setup-decision top-option logging behavior, and probability floor for non-setup decisions.

name: test_players_spec.py
summary: Tests for player spec parsing: verifies human/random specs have no checkpoint paths, named specs resolve against the checkpoint directory, run-directory specs seat the last checkpoint, and .pt-path specs derive the setup-net directory correctly.

name: test_powers_brown_activate.py
summary: Tests for brown bird powers that activate other birds' powers on trigger conditions, verifying correct dispatch and effect application within the engine.

name: test_powers_brown_bonus.py
summary: Tests for brown bird powers that award bonus cards or scoring bonuses, checking that the engine correctly handles the bonus card mechanics.

name: test_powers_brown_cache.py
summary: Tests for brown bird cache powers (cache food/eggs on birds), verifying the item is placed correctly on the triggered bird and state is updated.

name: test_powers_brown_discard.py
summary: Tests for brown bird discard powers that require the player to discard a card in exchange for a benefit, verifying decision flow and state changes.

name: test_powers_brown_draw.py
summary: Tests for brown bird draw-card powers, verifying that the correct number of cards are drawn from the deck or tray and added to the player's hand.

name: test_powers_brown_egg.py
summary: Tests for brown bird egg-laying powers, verifying the correct number of eggs are placed on the correct target birds after the power triggers.

name: test_powers_brown_food.py
summary: Tests for brown bird food-gain powers, verifying that the correct food types are added to the player's supply after the power triggers.

name: test_powers_brown_play.py
summary: Tests for brown bird play-bird powers that allow playing additional birds at reduced cost, verifying the decision sequence and board placement.

name: test_powers_misc_unique.py
summary: Comprehensive test suite for miscellaneous unique bird powers including draw-from-tray-all, trade-wild-food, fewest-habitat-gains-die, draw-n-plus-one-draft, all-players-draw, predator hunt, move-bird, and repeat-predator-power mechanics.

name: test_powers_pink_reactive.py
summary: Tests for pink (when-activated) reactive bird powers including per-use firing caps, veto/accept/decline flows, and lay-egg reactor targeting with exclude-self semantics.

name: test_powers_tuck_conditional.py
summary: Tests for conditional tuck powers including tuck-then-draw, tuck-then-lay-on-this, tuck-then-gain-food, and tuck-from-hand mechanics, verifying accept/skip branches and state transitions.

name: test_report_panels.py
summary: Tests for HTML training report panels, verifying section/arch panel visibility defaults, SVG click hooks, and overall HTML report generation structure.

name: test_round_goal_coverage.py
summary: Tests ensuring all 16 round goal types have registered scorers and that coverage gaps are detected during integration runs.

name: test_smoke.py
summary: Smoke test that runs a complete random game to completion, verifying the engine can execute an end-to-end game without errors.

name: test_decision_families.py
summary: Tests that decision classes are grouped into the correct family buckets used for per-decision-model routing, validating that food, card, and action decisions belong to their expected families.

name: test_decision_shapes.py
summary: Tests for decision shape and filtering logic: verifies that star-nests count for eligible-bird queries, egg-laying decisions correctly filter by nest type and egg capacity, and veto/accept gates apply proper constraints.

name: test_discard_egg_for_wild.py
summary: Tests for the discard-egg-for-wild-food bird power, covering parser recognition of all five applicable birds, correct egg removal and food grant, no-op when no eligible eggs exist, and skippable behavior.

name: test_dynamic_bonus_scoring.py
summary: Tests for dynamic bonus card scoring at end of game, including ecologist-style fewest-habitat cards and integration of dynamic bonus scores into final scoring totals.

name: test_egg_goal_delta.py
summary: Tests for egg-related round goal delta pricing logic: verifies that goal-delta values are correctly computed for eggs-in-habitat, birds-with-eggs, egg-sets, and no-eggs goals with threshold crossing semantics.

name: test_encode.py
summary: Comprehensive test suite for the state and choice encoder, verifying POV rotation, card identity offsets, decision type one-hots, feeder flags, board/tray encoding, bonus delta pricing, and goal delta computation across all encoder stripe regions.

name: test_encode_spec.py
summary: Tests for the encoder layout specification, verifying that card region offsets are spec-invariant, stripe layout sums match expected dimensions, and round-trip reconstruction of include-setup configuration is consistent.

name: test_setup_model.py
summary: Tests for setup-phase model integration, verifying that the actor-critic model handles setup decisions correctly during self-play initialization.

name: test_setup_self_play.py
summary: Tests for setup-phase self-play mechanics, verifying that model-based agents correctly handle the initial bird and bonus card selection decisions.

name: test_tournament_dashboard.py
summary: Tests the tournament dashboard rendering module, verifying that the live state panel and model-picker UI render correctly for empty, populated, and interactive states.

name: test_tournament_discovery.py
summary: Tests the participant/run discovery logic, covering active and archived run detection, graceful handling of unreadable checkpoints, empty directories, and rejection of stale-encoded runs.

name: test_tournament_elo.py
summary: Tests the ELO rating system used by the tournament, covering expected-score symmetry, zero-sum update invariants, and order-independence of batch replays.

name: test_tournament_results.py
summary: Tests the tournament results aggregation module, verifying matchup bucketing by seat, per-participant record and ELO ordering, and JSON round-trip serialization of TournamentReport.

name: test_tournament_schedule.py
summary: Tests the tournament schedule builder, verifying total game counts, balanced seating across all pairs, shared deal seeds for mirrored orientations, and A-seat orientation tracking.

name: test_version.py
summary: Tests the artifact versioning system, covering parse_version, check_artifact_compatible compatibility enforcement, adapt_encoding_for_version validation, and ModelConfig JSON round-trips with and without explicit version fields.
